title: calystegia sepium var. japonica
intro: Calystegia sepium var. japonica, also known as the Japanese bindweed or Japanese morning glory, is a perennial plant in the Convolvulaceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits associated with Calystegia sepium var. japonica include its potential to reduce stress, improve sleep quality, and alleviate symptoms of menopause, as well as its use as a natural diuretic. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
